title: Simple, Informative Redstone
description: Simpler textures for redstone, while providing valuable information.
body:
![Simple, Informative Redstone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c041d64a01dd0b0fe220e67dac3355fd8cb7ed47.png)

The Simple, Informative Redstone resource pack makes circuits easier to look at (with simpler textures), while simultaneously providing valuable information, such as signal strength, block states, block directions, and blocks made more distinguishable from eachother. I hope yall enjoy it!

*************** READ BEFORE DOWNLOADING *************** 

There are two versions of this pack. v2.1.1.1 is the base version, modifying all the redstone textures and providing information. v2.1.2.1 is the same, but also modifies the textures of wool, concrete, and terracotta to make counting in circuits easier. Download the version for the textures you want. I personally keep v2.1.2.1 on most the time, but have v2.1.1.1 available if I don't want wool, concrete, and terracotta to have modified textures at the time.


########## Textures Changed #########
- Redstone Dust
  - signal strength display when active
- Redstone Block
- Redstone Torch
- Redstone Repeater
- Redstone Comparator
  - equals sign when in compare mode
  - minus sign when in subtract mode
- Redstone Lamp
- Piston
- Sticky Piston
- slime ball icon to make distinguishable from regular piston
- Observer
  - arrows indicating direction of redstone current
- Dispenser
  - arrows indicating direction dispenser facing
  - bow icon to make distinguishable from dropper
- Dropper
  - arrows indicating direction dropper facing
- Crafter
- Hopper
  - arrows indicating direction hopper flows
  - red outline when locked
- Sculk Sensor
  - signal strength output
- Calibrated Sculk Sensor
  - signal strength output
- Target Block
  - signal strength output
- Note Block
  - number and note
- Jukebox
  - disc icon when disc is inside
- Daylight Detector
  - signal strength output
- Cauldron
  - signal strength from comparator
- Composter
  - signal strength from comparator
- Buttons
  - 2x2 red pixels on stone, when pressed, indicating shorter signal length
  - 2x4 red pixels on wood, when pressed, indicating longer signal
  - all as overlay, so other packs can render underneath
- Pressure Plates
  - red ring on regular plates, when pressed, indicating longer signal
  - red dot on weighted plates, when pressed, indicating shorter signal
  - all as overlay, so other packs can render underneath
- Lever
  - dark red strip, indicating no signal
  - light red strip, indicating active signal
  - all as overlay, so other packs can render underneath
- Slime Block
- Honey Block
- Trapped Chest
  - dark red outline to stand out from standard chests
- Wool
  - outline to make counting easier
- Concrete
  - outline to make counting easier
- Terracotta
  - outline to make counting easier
